name: Circuit
description_short: Circuit FCU is a member-owned credit union serving Western New York communities with personal loans, credit cards, mortgages, and deposit accounts at competitive rates.
description_long:
Circuit Federal Credit Union (formerly Cheektowaga Central FCU, then Cheektowaga Community FCU) has evolved significantly since its inception. On January 1, 2024, it rebranded to Circuit FCU and expanded membership eligibility to residents, workers, and students across eight Western New York towns including Hamburg, Cheektowaga, Blasdell, Orchard Park, West Seneca, Depew, Lancaster, and Sloan. The credit union recently opened a new Hamburg location at 140 Pine Street, Unit 3A, demonstrating active growth.

Circuit FCU offers a comprehensive range of consumer financial services including personal loans up to $15,000 (at rates as low as 9.39% APR as of April 2026), RV loans (as low as 5.59% APR), credit cards (8.99% APR and 10.99% APR for balance transfers), mortgage services, checking and savings accounts, and share certificates. The credit union also provides modern digital banking features including mobile deposit through their mobile app, check reordering, and access to the AllPoint ATM network. Members can obtain personalized mortgage quotes and apply for loans online.

Circuit FCU distinguishes itself through its military and first responder discount program, offering 0.25% off regular interest rates for active or retired military, law enforcement, and firefighters (with proper ID verification). As a credit union rather than a bank, it operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned basis, meaning earnings benefit members rather than shareholders. The organization welcomes member feedback and maintains transparent communication through newsletters and announcements.

The primary limitation is geographic restriction—membership is limited to those who live, work, worship, or attend school in specific Western New York towns, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this region. Website content lacks detailed information about minimum deposit requirements (beyond the $25 voting requirement), comprehensive fee schedules, and specific eligibility criteria for loans. Rates advertised are stated as 'based on credit worthiness,' meaning individual rates may vary significantly.
services: Personal loans up to $15,000 for 72 months
Credit cards with promotional balance transfer rates
Military and first responder discount program
pros: Military and first responder discount of 0.25% off regular interest rates with proper ID verification
Personal loans up to $15,000 available for 72 months at rates as low as 9.39% APR
RV loans offered at competitive rates as low as 5.59% APR
Mobile deposit feature launched on mobile app for convenient check deposits
Not-for-profit credit union structure means earnings benefit members, not external shareholders
Expanded membership eligibility as of January 2023 to eight Western New York towns
Access to AllPoint ATM network for widespread cash withdrawal options
cons: Geographic restriction limits membership to specific Western New York towns only—not available nationwide
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, monthly maintenance charges, and other account costs
Advertised rates are 'based on credit worthiness,' meaning actual rates may be significantly higher than advertised minimums
Limited branch network with only two locations mentioned (Hamburg opened recently; original location not specified)
Website error message suggests potential technical issues with online account access
best_for: Western New York residents, workers, and students seeking member-owned credit union benefits with competitive rates
Active military, retired military, law enforcement, and firefighters eligible for the 0.25% interest rate discount
Consumers seeking RV financing or personal loans in the $1,000–$15,000 range
Members preferring not-for-profit financial institutions over for-profit banks
diagnosis: Circuit FCU is best suited for Western New York residents, particularly military and first responder communities, seeking competitive personal loans, mortgages, and credit card services from a not-for-profit credit union. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership restrictions limiting access to eight specific towns in Western New York, making it unsuitable for consumers outside this service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Circuit — NCUA-insured credit union in CHEEKTOWAGA, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
